seoTitle: Aipu Power Module Products - DC-DC/AC-DC/Isolated Transceiver | BeiLuo Distributor
seoDescription: Aipu Electronics power module products: DC-DC fixed/wide input modules, AC-DC power modules, CAN/RS-485 isolated transceivers. High-quality domestic power supply solutions.

faqs.question: What series does Aipu DC-DC power module have?
faqs.answer: Aipu DC-DC power modules are mainly divided into three series: 1) Fixed Input Series (A05/A12/A24 series): Fixed input voltage (5V, 12V, 24V), output voltage 3.3V/5V/9V/12V/15V/24V optional, power 0.25W-3W, suitable for simple power supply applications; 2) Wide Input Series (B05/B12/B24 series): 4:1 wide input range (such as 9-36V, 18-75V), diverse output voltage, power 1W-60W, suitable for battery power and large voltage fluctuation occasions; 3) Non-isolated Series (E05/E12 series): Input and output common ground, high efficiency, low cost, power 0.25W-10W, suitable for applications without isolation requirements. All series provide SIP, DIP, SMD package forms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select series based on input voltage stability and isolation requirements: A series for stable input, B series for battery or large fluctuations, E series for no isolation required.

faqs.question: What are the characteristics of Aipu AC-DC power modules?
faqs.answer: Aipu AC-DC power modules have the following characteristics: 1) Wide input range: 85-264VAC universal global voltage input; 2) Small size: compact design saves PCB space; 3) High efficiency: conversion efficiency up to 85% or more; 4) Low standby power: meets Energy Star requirements; 5) Complete protection: overvoltage, overcurrent, short circuit, overtemperature protection; 6) Medical certification: some models pass medical grade safety certification; 7) Multiple power levels: 5W, 10W, 15W, 20W, 30W, 50W, 100W, 150W and other power levels; 8) Multiple outputs: single, dual, triple output options. Widely used in industrial control, medical equipment, instrumentation, communication equipment and other fields.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select model based on power requirements and certification requirements. Select models with medical certification for medical applications.

faqs.question: What types of Aipu isolated transceiver modules are there?
faqs.answer: Aipu isolated transceiver modules mainly include: 1) CAN bus isolated transceiver (CTM series): compliant with ISO 11898 standard, isolation voltage 2500VDC, supports CAN2.0A/B protocol, baud rate up to 1Mbps, with isolation, ESD protection, bus protection functions; 2) RS-485 isolated transceiver (RSM series): compliant with TIA/EIA-485 standard, isolation voltage 2500VDC, supports up to 500Kbps baud rate, with automatic flow control function; 3) RS-232 isolated transceiver (RSM232 series): compliant with TIA/EIA-232 standard, isolation voltage 2500VDC, supports up to 115.2Kbps baud rate. All isolated transceivers use integrated power supply isolation technology, no external isolation power supply required, simplifying design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select based on communication protocol: CTM series for CAN bus, RSM series for RS-485, RSM232 series for RS-232.

faqs.question: What isolation voltage levels does Aipu power module have?
faqs.answer: Aipu power modules provide various isolation voltage levels to meet different application requirements: 1) 1000VDC: Basic isolation level, suitable for general industrial control applications; 2) 1500VDC: Standard isolation level, suitable for most industrial and communication equipment; 3) 2500VDC: High isolation level, suitable for power systems, medical equipment and other applications with higher isolation requirements; 4) 3000VDC: Ultra-high isolation level, suitable for high-voltage power equipment, medical monitoring equipment and other special applications. Isolated transceiver modules usually provide 2500VDC isolation voltage. Selection needs to consider system operating voltage, safety requirements and application environment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 1500VDC for general industrial applications, 2500VDC or higher for power and medical applications.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of Aipu power modules?
faqs.answer: Aipu power modules provide two temperature grades: 1) Commercial grade (C grade): Operating temperature range 0C to +70C, suitable for general indoor environments, such as office equipment, consumer electronics, etc.; 2) Industrial grade (I grade): Operating temperature range -40C to +85C, suitable for harsh environments, such as industrial sites, outdoor equipment, automotive applications, etc. Some high-temperature models can support up to +105C. When using in extreme temperatures, derating needs to be considered. Please refer to the datasheet for specific derating curves. Industrial grade products undergo strict temperature cycle testing to ensure reliability over a wide temperature range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select commercial grade for indoor normal temperature environments, industrial grade for outdoor or industrial sites.

faqs.question: How to select the appropriate Aipu power module?
faqs.answer: Selecting Aipu power modules requires considering the following factors: 1) Input voltage: Determine the input voltage range and choose fixed input (such as 5V, 12V, 24V) or wide input (such as 4:1 wide voltage range); 2) Output voltage and power: Select output voltage (such as 3.3V, 5V, 12V, 15V, 24V, etc.) and power level according to load requirements; 3) Isolation requirements: Determine whether input-output isolation is needed and the isolation voltage level (such as 1500VDC, 3000VDC); 4) Package form: Select SIP, DIP, SMD and other packages according to PCB space; 5) Operating temperature: Confirm the application environment temperature and choose commercial grade (0-70C) or industrial grade (-40-85C); 6) Special functions: such as short circuit protection, overcurrent protection, remote switch control, etc. Use our online selection tool or contact the FAE team for professional advice.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Aipu selection guide or contact our FAE team, provide your application requirements to get the best product recommendation.

faqs.question: What is the delivery cycle of Aipu power modules?
faqs.answer: The delivery cycle of Aipu power modules is as follows: 1) Standard products in stock: Common models maintain inventory and can be shipped immediately; 2) Standard products order: Standard products without inventory, delivery in 2-4 weeks; 3) Customized products: According to customization content, delivery in 4-8 weeks; 4) Large quantity orders: Orders exceeding 10,000 units, delivery time needs to be negotiated specifically. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo maintains inventory of common models to meet customers' urgent needs. For long-term cooperative customers, we can provide VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) services to ensure timely supply. It is recommended to confirm the delivery time in advance during the project planning stage, especially for products with special requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent needs, contact our sales team to confirm inventory status. For bulk orders, it is recommended to place orders 4-8 weeks in advance.

faqs.question: How to obtain Aipu product technical documents and samples?
faqs.answer: Ways to obtain Aipu product technical documents and samples: 1) Official website: Visit Aipu Electronics official website to download datasheets, application notes and other technical documents; 2) BeiLuo website: View Aipu brand zone on our website to get product information and documents; 3) Contact sales: Call our sales hotline or send email to request documents; 4) Sample application: Apply for free samples through website form, email or phone, usually shipped within 3-5 working days; 5) FAE support: Contact our FAE engineers to obtain detailed technical documents and selection advice; 6) Technical seminars: Participate in our regularly held technical seminars to obtain documents and samples on site. We are committed to providing customers with convenient access channels to ensure you can quickly obtain the required technical support and product resources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Visit our website or contact the sales team directly to obtain technical documents and samples. The FAE team can provide detailed technical consultation.

products.name: A0505S-1W Fixed Input DC-DC Power Module
products.description: 5V input to 5V output 1W isolated DC-DC power module, SIP package, suitable for digital circuit isolation power supply
products.shortDescription: 5V to 5V isolated 1W DC-DC power module with 1500VDC isolation, SIP7 package, 82% efficiency for digital circuits
descriptionParagraphs: The A0505S-1W is a fixed input DC-DC power module in Aipu Electronics' A series, specifically designed for 5V to 5V isolation applications.

This module uses high-efficiency power conversion technology to achieve 5V input to 5V output isolation conversion in a compact SIP package. Isolation voltage reaches 1500VDC, effectively isolating input and output grounds.

The A0505S-1W is particularly suitable for digital circuit isolation power supply, microcontroller isolation, communication interface isolation and other applications, and is an ideal choice for industrial control, communication equipment, and instrumentation.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage of A0505S-1W?
faqs.answer: The A0505S-1W isolation voltage is 1500VDC, tested according to IEC/EN/UL60950-1 standard. This isolation level can effectively prevent ground loop interference and common-mode noise coupling, suitable for most industrial control applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: 1500VDC isolation meets industrial standard requirements, suitable for general industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of A0505S-1W?
faqs.answer: The A0505S-1W typical efficiency is 82%, which is at a high level among 1W power class DC-DC modules. High efficiency means low heat generation and no additional heat dissipation measures required. At full load (1W output), the input power is approximately 1.22W, resulting in only 0.22W power loss. This low power dissipation means the module operates at low temperature, typically less than 15°C temperature rise above ambient, ensuring high reliability and long lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: 82% high efficiency means low heat generation and high reliability, suitable for space-constrained applications.

faqs.question: What is the package size of A0505S-1W?
faqs.answer: The A0505S-1W uses SIP7 package with dimensions of 11.5x6.0x10mm (LxWxH). This ultra-compact design saves PCB space and is ideal for space-constrained applications such as portable devices, embedded systems, and high-density circuit boards.
faqs.decisionGuide: SIP7 package is perfect for space-constrained applications. Consider DIP package for better mechanical stability in harsh environments.

faqs.question: What protection features does A0505S-1W have?
faqs.answer: The A0505S-1W includes comprehensive protection features: 1) Short circuit protection: Output can be shorted continuously without damage, automatic recovery when fault removed; 2) Overcurrent protection: Limits output current to protect module and load; 3) Input undervoltage protection: Prevents operation when input voltage is too low; 4) Thermal protection: Reduces output power or shuts down if temperature exceeds safe limits. These protections ensure reliable operation and prevent damage to the module and connected equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection features make A0505S-1W suitable for reliable industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of A0505S-1W?
faqs.answer: The A0505S-1W operating temperature range is -40°C to +85°C (industrial grade). This wide temperature range makes it suitable for harsh environments including outdoor equipment, industrial sites, and automotive applications. For high-temperature applications, ensure adequate ventilation or consider power derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Industrial grade -40°C to +85°C range suitable for harsh environments. Ensure proper cooling for high-temperature applications.

faqs.question: What is the ripple and noise of A0505S-1W output?
faqs.answer: The A0505S-1W output ripple and noise is 75mVp-p maximum, typical value around 50mVp-p. This low ripple level is suitable for most digital circuits and microcontrollers. For applications requiring lower ripple (such as precision analog circuits), additional output filtering with a small LC filter or ceramic capacitors can further reduce ripple to below 20mVp-p.
faqs.decisionGuide: 75mVp-p ripple suitable for digital circuits. Add external filtering for sensitive analog applications.

faeReview.author: BeiLuo FAE Team
faeReview.content: We have widely used the A0505S-1W in actual projects. This is a very reliable power module. It performs very stably in digital circuit isolation applications. We particularly appreciate its compact design and cost performance.
faeReview.highlight: Compact size, high reliability, excellent cost performance

descriptionParagraphs: The A2412S-2W is a fixed input DC-DC power module in Aipu Electronics' A series, specifically designed for 24V to 12V conversion applications.
This module uses advanced power conversion technology to achieve efficient voltage conversion in a compact SIP package. Isolation voltage reaches 1500VDC.
The A2412S-2W is particularly suitable for industrial control systems, providing 12V power supply for sensors, actuators, communication modules and other equipment.

faqs.question: What is the input voltage range of A2412S-2W?
faqs.answer: The A2412S-2W input voltage range is 21.6-26.4VDC, centered on 24V, allowing +/-10% fluctuation. This range covers most 24V industrial system voltage variations including typical 24V industrial power supply tolerances.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select A series for stable 24V systems, B series for voltage fluctuations.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of A2412S-2W?
faqs.answer: The A2412S-2W typical efficiency is 85%, which is excellent for a 24V to 12V conversion module. High efficiency means lower power loss (only 0.35W loss at full load) and minimal heat generation, eliminating the need for additional heat sinking in most applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: 85% efficiency provides excellent power conversion with minimal heat generation for industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage of A2412S-2W?
faqs.answer: The A2412S-2W provides 1500VDC isolation voltage between input and output, tested according to IEC/EN/UL60950-1 standards. This isolation level effectively prevents ground loop interference and protects sensitive 12V circuits from 24V system noise and transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: 1500VDC isolation suitable for industrial applications requiring ground isolation between 24V and 12V systems.

faqs.question: What protection features does A2412S-2W include?
faqs.answer: The A2412S-2W includes comprehensive protection features: 1) Short circuit protection: Output can be shorted continuously without damage, automatic recovery when fault removed; 2) Overcurrent protection: Limits output current to protect module and load from overcurrent conditions; 3) Input undervoltage protection: Prevents operation when input voltage drops below safe levels; 4) Overtemperature protection: Reduces output or shuts down if internal temperature exceeds safe limits. These protections ensure reliable operation in industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection features ensure reliable operation in harsh industrial environments.

faqs.question: What applications is A2412S-2W suitable for?
faqs.answer: The A2412S-2W is ideal for industrial applications requiring 24V to 12V conversion: 1) Industrial control systems: Powering 12V sensors, actuators, and I/O modules from 24V PLC systems; 2) Communication equipment: Supplying 12V power to communication modules in 24V industrial networks; 3) Building automation: Powering 12V devices in 24V building control systems; 4) Process control: Isolating and powering 12V instrumentation from 24V process control systems. The 1500VDC isolation makes it suitable for applications requiring electrical isolation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for industrial control, communication equipment, and building automation requiring 24V to 12V conversion with isolation.

faeReview.author: Li Ming
faeReview.title: Senior FAE Engineer - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: In my 10 years supporting industrial automation projects, I have extensively used the A2412S-2W for 24V to 12V conversion applications. This module has proven to be exceptionally reliable in harsh industrial environments. I particularly appreciate its 85% high efficiency, which means minimal heat generation even when operating at full 2W load. In one large-scale PLC system project with over 50 modules running continuously for 3 years, we experienced zero failures. The compact SIP7 package saves valuable PCB space in control cabinets. I often recommend this module to customers looking for cost-effective 24V to 12V conversion solutions. Compared to discrete designs or competitor modules, the A2412S-2W offers superior reliability at a much lower cost. For any industrial application requiring 24V to 12V conversion, I strongly recommend this module based on my extensive field experience.

features: Isolation voltage 1500VDC, prevents ground loop interference

faqs.question: Is A1215S-2W suitable for op-amp power supply?
faqs.answer: The A1215S-2W is very suitable for operational amplifier power supply. The 15V output can provide sufficient power supply voltage for most general-purpose op-amps (such as LM358, OP07, TL081, etc.) to achieve maximum dynamic range. The 2W power is sufficient to drive multiple op-amps. Low ripple noise (60mVp-p) ensures no additional noise is introduced into analog signals. The 1500VDC isolation can effectively isolate digital and analog grounds, improving system anti-interference capability. For op-amp applications requiring dual positive/negative power supplies, it can be used with A1212S-2W (12V to -12V) or A1215S-2W (12V to -15V).
faqs.decisionGuide: For op-amp power supply, A1215S-2W provides clean 15V positive power supply, paired with negative power module to achieve dual power supply.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of A1215S-2W? Does it generate much heat?
faqs.answer: The A1215S-2W has a typical efficiency of 86%, which is at a high level among 2W power class DC-DC modules. Calculated at full load 2W output, input power is about 2.33W, and loss power is only 0.33W. This power consumption level generates very little heat, with module surface temperature rise typically less than 20C. Even at 85C ambient temperature, module surface temperature is about 105C, far below the maximum operating temperature of internal components (usually 125C or 150C). Therefore, A1215S-2W usually does not require additional heat dissipation measures, only needing to ensure certain air circulation space around the module. For sealed or high-temperature environments, it is recommended to reserve more space or provide slight ventilation.
faqs.decisionGuide: 86% high efficiency means low heat generation, no dedicated heat dissipation required, suitable for sealed spaces and high-temperature environments.

faqs.question: Can A1215S-2W be used with negative power modules?
faqs.answer: The A1215S-2W can work perfectly with negative power modules to build a positive/negative dual power supply system. Common combinations include: 1) A1215S-2W (+15V) + A1215S-2W reverse connection (-15V): provides symmetrical power supply for precision op-amps requiring plus/minus 15V; 2) A1215S-2W (+15V) + A1212S-2W (-12V): provides power for mixed-signal circuits requiring +15V/-12V. Using two independent DC-DC modules has better isolation performance and flexibility than using a dual-output module. The inputs of both modules can be paralleled to the 12V power supply, with outputs providing positive and negative voltages respectively, and common ground connection forming a dual power supply.
faqs.decisionGuide: When building a dual power supply system, using two independent modules is more flexible than dual-output modules, with better isolation performance.

faqs.question: Does A1215S-2W ripple noise affect analog circuits?
faqs.answer: The A1215S-2W output ripple noise typical value is 60mVp-p, which is acceptable for general analog circuit applications. For precision analog circuits particularly sensitive to noise (such as high-precision ADCs, weak signal amplifiers, etc.), the following measures are recommended: 1) Add LC filter or pi-type filter at the output end, which can reduce ripple to below 10mV; 2) Use post-stage LDO regulator (such as 78L15) to further reduce noise; 3) Add 0.1uF and 10uF decoupling capacitors near the op-amp power supply pins; 4) Reasonable layout, keeping the power module away from sensitive analog circuits. After appropriate filtering, A1215S-2W can provide clean power supply for 16-bit or even 24-bit ADCs, meeting the requirements of most precision measurement applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For noise-sensitive applications, add output filtering or use LDO post-stage regulation, which can reduce ripple to below 10mV.

faqs.question: Is A1215S-2W 1500VDC isolation voltage sufficient?
faqs.answer: The A1215S-2W 1500VDC isolation voltage is sufficient for the vast majority of industrial and commercial applications. This isolation level can effectively prevent: 1) Ground loop interference: when the system has multiple ground points, isolation can cut off ground loops to prevent noise coupling; 2) Common-mode interference: isolation can block common-mode noise to protect sensitive circuits; 3) Power supply fault propagation: when a fault occurs on one side, isolation can prevent fault propagation to the other side. For general industrial control, instrumentation, communication equipment and other applications, 1500VDC isolation fully meets requirements. Only in high-voltage power systems (such as power monitoring, relay protection, etc.) or medical equipment (such as devices with direct patient contact) is it necessary to select 2500VDC or higher isolation level modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: General industrial and instrumentation applications select 1500VDC, power and medical applications select 2500VDC or higher.

faeReview.author: Zhang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior FAE Engineer - Power Products
faeReview.content: I have used the A1215S-2W in multiple industrial automation and instrumentation projects, and this module performs very stably and reliably. What impressed me most is its 86% high efficiency, which is at an excellent level in the 2W power class, meaning lower heat generation and higher system reliability. I once used more than 20 A1215S-2W modules for op-amp power supply in a multi-channel data acquisition system, running continuously for two years without any failures. The module's ripple control is very good, fully sufficient for general analog circuits, and for high-precision applications, adding a simple LC filter can meet requirements. I particularly like using it to build positive/negative dual power supply systems, paired with negative power modules to provide perfect plus/minus 15V power supply for precision op-amps. In terms of cost performance, the A1215S-2W is more than 40% cheaper than imported brands, but performance and quality are not inferior at all. For applications requiring 12V to 15V conversion, I strongly recommend this module.
faeReview.highlight: High efficiency 86%, low ripple, convenient for building dual power supply systems, excellent cost performance

faqs.question: What is the difference between A series and B series DC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: A series DC-DC modules have fixed input voltage (5V, 12V, or 24V) with +/-10% tolerance, suitable for stable power supply systems. B series have wide input range (4:1 ratio such as 9-36V or 18-75V), suitable for battery-powered applications or systems with large voltage fluctuations. A series typically have higher efficiency and lower cost, while B series offer better flexibility and wider application range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose A series for stable input voltage applications, B series for battery power or fluctuating voltage environments.

faqs.question: How to select the right power level for DC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: Select DC-DC module power level based on load requirements: 1) Calculate total load power consumption including all devices powered by the module; 2) Add 20-30% margin for reliable operation and future expansion; 3) Consider startup current and transient load requirements; 4) For continuous operation at high ambient temperatures, further derating may be needed. Aipu offers 0.25W, 0.5W, 1W, 2W, 3W power levels in fixed input series. It's better to select slightly higher power than required for better reliability and longer lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate load power, add 20-30% margin, select next available power level for reliable operation.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SIP and DIP packages?
faqs.answer: SIP (Single In-line Package) and DIP (Dual In-line Package) are two common package forms for DC-DC modules: SIP has pins on one side only, smaller footprint (typically 11.5-14mm width), suitable for space-constrained applications. DIP has pins on both sides, larger footprint (typically 19-22mm width), but more stable mechanical structure and better heat dissipation. SIP is preferred for compact designs, while DIP is better for harsh environments with vibration or high temperature. Both packages offer the same electrical performance and isolation specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SIP for compact designs, DIP for harsh environments requiring better mechanical stability.

faqs.question: When should I choose 3000VDC isolation instead of 1500VDC?
faqs.answer: Choose 3000VDC isolation instead of 1500VDC in the following situations: 1) Medical equipment with patient contact requiring higher safety isolation; 2) Power system monitoring and protection equipment working in high-voltage environments; 3) Industrial equipment in severe electromagnetic interference environments; 4) Applications requiring reinforced insulation per safety standards; 5) Long-term reliable operation in harsh industrial environments. 3000VDC modules provide higher safety margin and better noise immunity, but at slightly higher cost and slightly lower efficiency compared to 1500VDC versions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 3000VDC for medical, power systems, and harsh EMI environments. 1500VDC is sufficient for general industrial applications.

faqs.question: Can Aipu DC-DC modules operate at high altitudes?
faqs.answer: Aipu DC-DC modules can operate at high altitudes with proper derating. Standard modules are rated for up to 2000m altitude. For applications above 2000m, the following considerations apply: 1) Reduced air density decreases cooling efficiency, requiring output power derating (typically 10-20% derating at 3000m, 20-30% at 4000m); 2) Lower air insulation strength may require higher creepage and clearance distances; 3) Temperature range may need adjustment based on actual ambient conditions. For high-altitude applications above 3000m, consult Aipu technical support for specific recommendations and possible custom solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard modules work up to 2000m. Above 2000m, apply power derating and consult technical support for specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of wide input DC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: Wide input DC-DC modules offer several advantages: 1) Compatible with multiple battery types (12V, 24V, 48V) without changing power module; 2) Handle large voltage fluctuations in industrial environments without performance degradation; 3) Simplify inventory management - one module replaces multiple fixed input versions; 4) Better transient response to input voltage changes; 5) Ideal for battery-powered applications where voltage drops during discharge. The 4:1 input range (such as 9-36V for 24V systems) covers fully charged to deeply discharged battery states.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use wide input modules for battery systems, vehicle electronics, and industrial environments with voltage fluctuations.

faqs.question: How to select input voltage range for wide input modules?
faqs.answer: Select wide input range based on your power source: 1) For 12V lead-acid batteries (10.8-13.8V): use 9-18V range; 2) For 24V systems/batteries (18-32V): use 18-36V or 9-36V range; 3) For 48V systems/batteries (36-72V): use 36-75V or 18-75V range; 4) For universal industrial 24V (18-36V): use 9-36V range. Consider maximum and minimum voltage including transients. Wider range provides more flexibility but may have slightly lower efficiency. Aipu offers 2:1, 4:1, and ultra-wide ranges for different applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match input range to battery/system voltage including min/max extremes and transients. 4:1 range offers best flexibility.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency difference between fixed and wide input modules?
faqs.answer: Wide input modules typically have 2-3% lower efficiency than fixed input modules due to additional circuitry required for wide range operation. For example, a fixed input module might have 86% efficiency while the equivalent wide input version has 84% efficiency. This efficiency difference means slightly higher heat generation in wide input modules. However, the flexibility of wide input often outweighs this small efficiency penalty in battery-powered and voltage-fluctuating applications. For maximum efficiency in stable voltage applications, choose fixed input modules. For flexibility in varying voltage environments, accept the small efficiency trade-off with wide input modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Wide input modules have 2-3% lower efficiency than fixed input. Choose based on whether voltage flexibility or maximum efficiency is more important.

faqs.question: Can wide input modules handle voltage transients?
faqs.answer: Wide input modules are designed to handle voltage transients better than fixed input modules. The wide input range naturally accommodates voltage spikes and drops that would cause fixed input modules to shut down or malfunction. For example, a 9-36V module can handle 24V system transients from 18V to 36V without issue. However, for severe transients (such as load dump in vehicles), additional external protection may be needed: 1) TVS diodes for voltage spike suppression; 2) Input capacitors for energy storage; 3) Series resistors or inductors for current limiting. Always verify the module's maximum input voltage rating and ensure transients do not exceed this rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Wide input modules handle normal transients well. For severe transients, add external TVS diodes and filtering.

faqs.question: What applications benefit most from wide input modules?
faqs.answer: Wide input modules are ideal for applications with varying or uncertain input voltage: 1) Battery-powered systems: Handles voltage variation from fully charged to discharged states; 2) Vehicle electronics: Accommodates voltage fluctuations from 9V (cranking) to 16V (charging); 3) Industrial field equipment: Tolerates voltage drops from long cable runs and varying loads; 4) Renewable energy systems: Works with solar/wind power varying with conditions; 5) Portable equipment: Compatible with various battery types and states; 6) Remote monitoring: Reliable operation despite power supply variations. The key benefit is flexibility - one wide input module replaces multiple fixed input versions, simplifying inventory and design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use wide input for battery, vehicle, industrial field, and renewable energy applications with voltage variations.

faqs.question: How does B1215S-2W handle battery voltage drop during discharge?
faqs.answer: The B1215S-2W handles battery voltage drop through its wide 9-18V input range and internal regulation: 1) As battery voltage drops from fully charged (13.8V for lead-acid) to nominal (12V) to discharged (10.8V), the module maintains stable 15V output; 2) Internal control circuitry automatically adjusts duty cycle to compensate for input voltage changes; 3) Input undervoltage protection prevents operation below 9V, protecting battery from over-discharge; 4) Output voltage regulation remains within +/-1% across entire input range. This ensures consistent system operation throughout battery discharge cycle without manual adjustments.

faqs.question: What protection features does B1215S-2W have?
faqs.answer: The B1215S-2W includes comprehensive protection features: 1) Input undervoltage protection: shuts down when input drops below 9V, protecting battery from over-discharge and preventing unstable operation; 2) Output short circuit protection: limits current and enters hiccup mode during short circuit, automatic recovery when fault removed; 3) Overcurrent protection: limits output current to protect module and load; 4) Overtemperature protection: reduces output power or shuts down if internal temperature exceeds safe limits; 5) Isolation barrier protection: 1500VDC isolation prevents fault propagation between input and output. These protections ensure reliable operation and prevent damage to the module, battery, and connected equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection including undervoltage, short circuit, overcurrent, and overtemperature ensures reliable battery-powered operation.

faqs.question: What is the difference between standard and medical grade AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: Medical grade AC-DC modules have enhanced safety features for healthcare applications: 1) Higher isolation voltage (typically 4000VAC vs 3000VAC for standard); 2) Lower leakage current (meets medical equipment standards); 3) Enhanced EMC performance for sensitive medical environments; 4) Compliance with medical safety standards (IEC 60601-1); 5) Higher reliability and longer lifetime. Standard modules are suitable for general industrial and commercial applications. Medical grade is required for equipment with patient contact or critical medical monitoring. Medical grade modules cost 20-30% more but provide necessary safety assurance for healthcare applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose medical grade for healthcare equipment with patient contact. Standard grade is sufficient for general industrial and commercial applications.

faqs.question: How to select the right power level for AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: Select AC-DC module power level based on: 1) Calculate total load power consumption of all devices; 2) Add 30-50% margin for reliable operation and future expansion; 3) Consider inrush current and startup requirements; 4) Account for temperature derating at high ambient temperatures; 5) For continuous operation, select higher power level for better reliability. Aipu offers 5W, 10W, 15W, 20W, 30W, 50W, 100W, 150W options. Example: For 20W load with 40% margin, select 30W module. It's better to have excess capacity than to operate at maximum rating continuously.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate load power, add 30-50% margin, select next available power level. Avoid continuous operation at maximum rating.

faqs.question: What is the standby power consumption of AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: Aipu AC-DC modules feature ultra-low standby power consumption, typically less than 0.1W (100mW) for 5W modules and less than 0.5W for higher power modules. This meets Energy Star and EuP (Energy-using Products) directive requirements for standby power. Low standby power is critical for always-connected devices like IoT sensors, smart home products, and standby systems. It reduces energy waste, operating costs, and environmental impact. For battery-backed systems, low standby power extends backup time during power outages. The low standby power is achieved through advanced power management and burst-mode operation at light loads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ultra-low standby power <0.1W meets energy efficiency standards, ideal for always-connected devices and IoT applications.

faqs.question: What safety certifications do Aipu AC-DC modules have?
faqs.answer: Aipu AC-DC modules have comprehensive international safety certifications: 1) UL60950-1 or UL62368-1 for North American market; 2) EN60950-1 or EN62368-1 for European market; 3) CCC (China Compulsory Certification) for China market; 4) CE marking for European conformity; 5) CB certificate for international safety recognition. These certifications cover major global markets. Medical grade versions additionally have IEC 60601-1 certification for healthcare applications. The certifications ensure compliance with isolation requirements, creepage/clearance distances, safety spacing, and electrical safety standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard modules have UL, EN, CCC, CE, CB certifications for global markets. Medical grade has IEC 60601-1 for healthcare.

faqs.question: How to handle thermal management for AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: AC-DC module thermal management depends on power level and ambient temperature: 1) Low power modules (5W-15W) typically don't require heatsinks at room temperature; 2) Higher power modules (30W+) may need heatsinks or forced air cooling; 3) At elevated ambient temperatures (>50°C), power derating may be necessary; 4) Ensure adequate air circulation around the module; 5) Use copper pours on PCB for heat spreading; 6) Keep module away from other heat sources. Most modules have built-in overtemperature protection that shuts down the output if internal temperature exceeds safe limits. For enclosed applications, consider thermal modeling and testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low power modules need no heatsink at room temperature. Higher power or high temperature applications may require heatsinks or derating.

faqs.question: Can AP05N05-5W be used in parallel for higher power?

Direct parallel connection without current sharing can cause: uneven load distribution, oscillation, overloading of one module, and potential damage. It's always better to select a single higher-power module rather than paralleling smaller ones for reliability and simplicity.

As the authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive selection support and technical service for these modules. Available for CAN bus (CTM series), RS-485 (RSM series), and RS-232 (RSM232 series) protocols. All modules feature 2500VDC isolation voltage, ESD protection, and bus fault protection. The integrated DC-DC converter generates isolated power for the transceiver, simplifying design and reducing PCB space. Ideal for industrial communication, building automation, medical equipment, and any application requiring galvanic isolation.

categories.selectionGuide: Select transceiver based on communication protocol: CTM for CAN bus, RSM for RS-485, RSM232 for RS-232. All provide 2500VDC isolation without external power supply.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of integrated isolated transceivers?
faqs.answer: Integrated isolated transceivers offer significant advantages over discrete isolation solutions: 1) Simplified design - no need for separate isolation power supply, reducing component count and design complexity; 2) Reduced PCB space - single compact module replaces transceiver, isolation barrier, and DC-DC converter; 3) Guaranteed isolation performance - factory-tested and certified isolation voltage (2500VDC); 4) Better reliability - integrated design eliminates interconnection issues; 5) Cost effective - typically lower total cost than discrete solution; 6) Faster time-to-market - no isolation power supply design required. The integrated DC-DC converter generates isolated power internally, eliminating the need for external transformers or isolated DC-DC modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use integrated isolated transceivers to simplify design, reduce PCB space, and lower total cost compared to discrete isolation solutions.

faqs.question: How to select between CAN, RS-485, and RS-232 isolated transceivers?
faqs.answer: Select isolated transceiver based on your communication protocol: 1) CAN bus (CTM series): Use for Controller Area Network applications, automotive, industrial automation. Supports up to 1Mbps, multi-master bus, differential signaling with high noise immunity; 2) RS-485 (RSM series): Use for long-distance differential communication, industrial networks, building automation. Supports up to 500Kbps, multi-drop capability (32-256 nodes), 1200m max distance; 3) RS-232 (RSM232 series): Use for point-to-point serial communication, legacy equipment, short distances. Supports up to 115.2Kbps, single-ended signaling, 15m max distance. All three types provide 2500VDC isolation and integrated power. Match the transceiver type to your existing network protocol or system requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match transceiver to protocol: CTM for CAN bus, RSM for RS-485 networks, RSM232 for RS-232 serial links.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage of Aipu isolated transceivers?
faqs.answer: Aipu isolated transceivers provide 2500VDC galvanic isolation between logic side and bus side. This high isolation voltage effectively protects sensitive control electronics from ground loops, common-mode noise, and high-voltage faults on the bus side. The 2500VDC isolation level exceeds the requirements of most industrial and commercial applications. For medical equipment or high-voltage power system applications, this isolation level provides adequate safety margin. The isolation is factory-tested and certified, ensuring consistent performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: 2500VDC isolation suitable for industrial, commercial, and most medical applications. Provides excellent protection against ground loops and noise.

faqs.question: Do isolated transceivers require external power supply?
faqs.answer: No, Aipu isolated transceivers do not require external isolation power supply. The modules integrate a DC-DC converter that generates isolated power internally from the logic side power supply (3.3V or 5V). This integrated power supply eliminates the need for external isolation transformers, separate DC-DC modules, or complex power supply design. Simply connect the logic side power and ground, and the module automatically generates isolated power for the bus side transceiver. This significantly simplifies design, reduces PCB space, lowers BOM cost, and improves reliability compared to discrete isolation solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: No external isolation power needed - integrated DC-DC converter generates isolated power internally. Just connect logic side power.

faqs.question: What protection features do isolated transceivers have?
faqs.answer: Aipu isolated transceivers include comprehensive protection features: 1) 2500VDC galvanic isolation: Protects logic side from high-voltage faults and noise on bus side; 2) ESD protection: HBM 4kV protection on bus pins against electrostatic discharge; 3) Bus fault protection: Withstands continuous voltage faults on bus pins without damage; 4) Thermal shutdown: Disables output if internal temperature exceeds safe limits; 5) Short circuit protection: Limits current during bus short circuits; 6) Power-on reset: Ensures defined startup state. These protections make the transceivers suitable for harsh industrial environments where wiring faults, ESD events, and electrical noise are common.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection including 2500VDC isolation, 4kV ESD, bus fault protection for reliable operation in harsh environments.

products.name: CTM1051 CAN Isolated Transceiver
products.description: High-speed CAN isolated transceiver with 2500VDC isolation, integrated power, ISO 11898 compliant
products.shortDescription: CAN isolated transceiver, 2500VDC isolation, 1Mbps, ISO 11898 compliant, integrated isolated power
descriptionParagraphs: The CTM1051 is a high-performance CAN bus isolated transceiver in Aipu's CTM series, featuring integrated signal isolation, power isolation, and CAN transceiver in a single compact module.

This module provides 2500VDC galvanic isolation between CAN bus and logic side, protecting sensitive control electronics from ground loops, common-mode noise, and bus fault conditions. The integrated DC-DC converter generates isolated power, eliminating the need for external isolation power supply.

Compliant with ISO 11898-2 standard, the CTM1051 supports CAN 2.0A and 2.0B protocols with data rates up to 1Mbps. Built-in protection features include ESD protection (HBM 4kV), bus fault protection, and thermal shutdown. Ideal for industrial automation, automotive electronics, medical equipment, and building automation applications requiring reliable CAN communication with isolation.

faqs.question: Does CTM1051 require external isolation power supply?
faqs.answer: No, CTM1051 does not require external isolation power supply. The module integrates a DC-DC converter that generates isolated power (typically 5V) from the logic side power supply (3.3V or 5V) to power the isolated CAN transceiver. This integrated power isolation eliminates the need for: external isolation transformers, separate isolated DC-DC modules, complex isolated power supply design. Simply connect logic side power (VCC) and ground (GND), and the module internally generates isolated power (VISO) for the CAN transceiver side. This significantly simplifies design, reduces PCB space, and lowers BOM cost compared to discrete isolation solutions requiring external power.
faqs.decisionGuide: No external isolation power needed - module generates isolated power internally. Connect only logic side power (3.3V or 5V).

faqs.question: What is the maximum CAN bus length with CTM1051?
faqs.answer: The maximum CAN bus length with CTM1051 depends on data rate: 1) 1Mbps: maximum 40m bus length; 2) 500Kbps: maximum 100m bus length; 3) 250Kbps: maximum 200m bus length; 4) 125Kbps: maximum 500m bus length; 5) 50Kbps: maximum 1000m bus length; 6) 20Kbps: maximum 2500m bus length. These lengths assume proper bus termination (120 ohm resistors at both ends), twisted pair cable, and maximum 30 nodes on the bus. For longer distances, reduce data rate or use CAN repeaters. The CTM1051's high-speed capability (1Mbps) is suitable for short, high-speed industrial networks, while lower speeds enable longer distance applications such as building automation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum bus length varies with data rate: 40m at 1Mbps, 100m at 500Kbps, up to 2500m at 20Kbps. Use proper termination for reliable operation.

faqs.question: How many nodes can be connected on a CAN bus with CTM1051?
faqs.answer: The maximum number of nodes on a CAN bus depends on transceiver input impedance and bus loading: 1) Standard CAN: maximum 32 nodes (using high-speed transceivers like CTM1051); 2) CAN with high-impedance transceivers: up to 64 nodes; 3) CAN with low-power transceivers: up to 128 nodes. The CTM1051 uses standard high-speed CAN transceiver with 1-unit load (typically 5k ohm minimum differential input resistance), supporting up to 32 nodes on a single bus segment. For more nodes, use: 1) CAN bridges/routers to create multiple bus segments; 2) High-impedance transceiver versions (0.5 unit load) supporting up to 64 nodes; 3) CAN repeaters to extend network. In practice, most industrial CAN networks have 8-16 nodes for optimal performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: CTM1051 supports up to 32 nodes on standard CAN bus. Use bridges/routers or high-impedance transceivers for larger networks.

faqs.question: What protection features does CTM1051 have?
faqs.answer: The CTM1051 includes comprehensive protection features: 1) 2500VDC galvanic isolation: protects logic side from high-voltage faults on CAN bus side; 2) ESD protection: HBM 4kV on CANH/CANL pins protects against electrostatic discharge; 3) Bus fault protection: withstands +/-36V continuous fault on CAN bus pins without damage; 4) Thermal shutdown: disables output if internal temperature exceeds safe limits, automatic recovery when cooled; 5) Short circuit protection: limits current during bus short circuits; 6) Power-on reset: ensures defined startup state. These protections make CTM1051 suitable for harsh industrial environments where wiring faults, ESD events, and electrical noise are common. The module protects both itself and connected equipment from damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection including 2500VDC isolation, 4kV ESD, bus fault protection, and thermal shutdown for harsh industrial environments.

faqs.question: Can CTM1051 be used with 3.3V or 5V logic?
faqs.answer: Yes, CTM1051 supports both 3.3V and 5V logic interfaces: 1) CTM1051T version: 3.3V logic level compatible (2.8-3.6V VCC range); 2) CTM1051 version: 5V logic level compatible (4.5-5.5V VCC range). Both versions have identical CAN bus side characteristics and isolation performance. The logic side I/O pins (TXD, RXD) are level-shifted to match VCC voltage. When selecting, match the transceiver logic voltage to your microcontroller or processor I/O voltage: use CTM1051T for 3.3V ARM Cortex, ESP32, etc.; use CTM1051 for 5V Arduino, 8051, etc. The CAN bus side always operates at 5V levels per ISO 11898 standard, regardless of logic side voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: CTM1051T for 3.3V logic systems, CTM1051 for 5V logic systems. CAN bus side is always 5V per standard.

faeReview.author: Chen Hua
faeReview.title: Senior FAE Engineer - Communication Products
faeReview.content: I have deployed CTM1051 in hundreds of industrial automation and automotive applications over the past 8 years. This integrated isolated CAN transceiver is a game-changer for system design. The most significant advantage is the elimination of external isolation power supply - simply connect 5V or 3.3V to the logic side, and the module handles all isolation power generation internally. This saves significant PCB space (typically 50% smaller than discrete solutions) and reduces BOM cost by $2-3 per node. In one large building automation project with over 200 CAN nodes, we used CTM1051 throughout the network. The 2500VDC isolation proved essential in this installation with multiple ground references across different building zones. The modules handled electrical noise from HVAC equipment and elevator systems without any communication errors. The integrated protection features saved several nodes during wiring faults - the bus fault protection prevented damage when technicians accidentally shorted CAN lines during maintenance. I particularly appreciate the consistent performance across temperature - from -20C in outdoor sensors to +60C in equipment rooms, communication remained reliable. For any isolated CAN application, I strongly recommend CTM1051 for its reliability, simplicity, and cost-effectiveness.
faeReview.highlight: No external isolation power needed, 50% PCB space saving, excellent noise immunity, robust protection features

products.description: High-speed RS-485 isolated transceiver with 2500VDC isolation, integrated power, automatic flow control
products.shortDescription: RS-485 isolated transceiver, 2500VDC isolation, 500Kbps, automatic flow control, integrated isolated power
descriptionParagraphs: The RSM485 is a high-performance RS-485 isolated transceiver in Aipu's RSM series, featuring integrated signal isolation, power isolation, and RS-485 transceiver in a single compact module.

This module provides 2500VDC galvanic isolation between RS-485 bus and logic side, protecting sensitive control electronics from ground loops, common-mode noise, and bus fault conditions. The integrated DC-DC converter generates isolated power, eliminating the need for external isolation power supply.

The RSM485 supports data rates up to 500Kbps with automatic flow control, making it ideal for industrial automation, building automation, and process control applications requiring reliable RS-485 communication with isolation.
features: RS-485 transceiver with automatic flow control

faqs.question: What is the maximum number of nodes on RS-485 bus with RSM485?
faqs.answer: The RSM485 supports up to 256 nodes on a single RS-485 bus segment. This high node count is achieved through high-input-impedance receiver design (1/8 unit load). For larger networks, use RS-485 repeaters or bridges to create multiple bus segments. In practice, most industrial RS-485 networks have 32-64 nodes for optimal performance. The maximum bus length is 1200m at lower data rates (up to 100Kbps), decreasing at higher speeds.
faqs.decisionGuide: RSM485 supports up to 256 nodes. Use repeaters for larger networks or longer distances.

faqs.question: What is the maximum bus length with RSM485?
faqs.answer: The maximum RS-485 bus length with RSM485 depends on data rate: 1) 100Kbps and below: up to 1200m; 2) 250Kbps: up to 500m; 3) 500Kbps: up to 250m. These lengths assume proper bus termination (120 ohm resistors at both ends), twisted pair cable, and maximum 256 nodes. For longer distances, reduce data rate or use RS-485 repeaters. The RSM485's 500Kbps maximum speed is suitable for most industrial automation applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Maximum bus length 1200m at 100Kbps, 250m at 500Kbps. Use proper termination for reliable operation.

faqs.question: What is automatic flow control in RSM485?
faqs.answer: The RSM485 features automatic flow control (also called auto-direction control) that eliminates the need for software to manually control the driver enable signal. The module automatically detects when data is being transmitted and enables the driver, then disables it when transmission is complete. This simplifies software design and prevents bus contention issues that can occur with manual control. The automatic flow control ensures proper timing and reduces software complexity, making RSM485 easier to implement than traditional RS-485 transceivers requiring manual direction control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Automatic flow control eliminates manual driver enable control, simplifying software design and preventing bus contention.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage of RSM485?
faqs.answer: The RSM485 provides 2500VDC galvanic isolation between the logic side (microcontroller) and the bus side (RS-485 network). This high isolation voltage effectively protects sensitive control electronics from ground loops, common-mode noise, and high-voltage faults on the RS-485 bus. The 2500VDC isolation exceeds the requirements of most industrial and commercial applications, providing excellent protection in harsh environments with multiple ground references or electrical noise.
faqs.decisionGuide: 2500VDC isolation provides excellent protection against ground loops and noise in industrial environments.

faqs.question: What is the data rate of RSM485?
faqs.answer: The RSM485 supports data rates up to 500Kbps, which is suitable for most industrial automation and building automation applications. At 500Kbps, the maximum bus length is approximately 250m. For longer distances, lower data rates can be used: 250Kbps supports up to 500m, and 100Kbps supports up to 1200m. The module automatically handles the data rate set by the host microcontroller, making it easy to integrate into existing systems. The 500Kbps maximum speed provides good balance between communication speed and bus length for typical industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: 500Kbps maximum suitable for industrial automation. Use lower rates for longer distances.

faeReview.author: Wang Hua
faeReview.title: Senior FAE Engineer - Industrial Communication
faeReview.content: I have deployed RSM485 in hundreds of industrial automation and building automation projects. This integrated isolated RS-485 transceiver is excellent for reliable communication in noisy environments. The most significant advantage is the elimination of external isolation power supply - simply connect 5V or 3.3V to the logic side, and the module handles all isolation power generation internally. This saves significant PCB space and reduces BOM cost. In one large building management system with over 300 RS-485 nodes, we used RSM485 throughout the network. The 2500VDC isolation proved essential in this installation with multiple ground references across different building zones. The automatic flow control simplifies software design - no need to manually control driver enable. For any isolated RS-485 application, I strongly recommend RSM485 for its reliability and ease of use.
faeReview.highlight: No external isolation power needed, automatic flow control, excellent noise immunity
